title: alchemilla hybrida subsp. flabellata
intro: Alchemilla hybrida subsp. flabellata, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herb in the buckwheat family, characterized by its attractive, fan-shaped leaves and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, lady's mantle has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla hybrida subsp. flabellata include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce menstrual cramps, and calm skin irritations, offering relief from conditions such as bloating, PMS symptoms, and eczema. Herbal preparations of lady's mantle include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal benefits.
